visual_features.overall_style: Clean, editorial, and somewhat classic. The design mixes a fashion-magazine aesthetic (large serif typography, full-bleed imagery) with a restrained corporate feel (simple navigation, minimal ornamentation). Color usage is minimal, with a single teal brand color, white, and grayscale photography, giving a sophisticated, high-end agency look.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven primarily by scale, contrast, and position. The largest element is the hero headline 'MORE THAN PRETTY SINCE 1990' in oversized white serif type centered on the screen, immediately drawing attention. Second-level elements are the logo block at the top left (high-contrast teal block with large serif logotype) and the navigation row across the top. The 'FRANÇAIS' language switch stands out with a teal background, creating a secondary focal point on the top right. Blurred grayscale imagery recedes into the background, ensuring text dominates.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: The layout uses generous whitespace in the header and around navigation items. The hero headline has large vertical spacing between its two lines and significant margin from the top, creating an airy, editorial feel. The clean white header bar provides strong visual breathing room between the navigation and the busy background image beneath. Overall spacing is comfortable rather than dense.
unique_elements: Large teal block logo with serif logotype and a small sans-serif tagline inside the block
Full-width blurred grayscale collage of model portraits as the hero background, creating texture without competing with text
Oversized, high-contrast serif headline centered on the hero, reminiscent of fashion magazine covers
Single prominent language-switch button styled in brand color in the main navigation

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Likely a 12-column responsive grid with a full-width container for the hero and a fixed max-width inner container for header content and navigation links.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Top-fixed horizontal header with logo on the left and navigation spanning center/right, followed by a full-bleed hero section with centered typography over a background image.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: The visible structure shows a clear division: (1) a white header bar containing logo and navigation, (2) a large hero section spanning the full viewport width with a background image and center-aligned headline. Subsequent sections (not visible) are likely stacked vertically beneath the hero.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Header content uses a horizontal row: logo left-aligned, nav items center/right-aligned on the same baseline, language button right-aligned. The hero headline is centrally aligned both horizontally and vertically within the hero area, with text center-aligned. Navigation links are evenly spaced, creating a strong horizontal alignment line.
responsive_hints: Full-width hero background suggests the image will crop responsively while maintaining center focus.
Single-row navigation likely collapses into a hamburger or stacked menu on smaller viewports.
Centered hero text indicates it will remain central on all device widths, potentially with reduced font sizes on mobile.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear, top-to-bottom flow. First, a horizontal header bar with a left-aligned logo block and a single row of navigation links stretching toward the right, capped by a solid-color language switch button. Directly beneath, a full-width hero section with a blurred grayscale image collage and large, two-line centered headline text. The hero uses a simple single-column content arrangement: background image layer, then centered headline overlay.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header occupies a relatively shallow vertical band at the top (roughly 10–15% of the viewport height), prioritizing horizontal space for logo and navigation. The hero section underneath takes the majority of the initial viewport height, giving prominence to the brand message. Within the hero, the headline occupies a central band with generous top and bottom margins, leaving substantial negative space above and below the copy for an uncluttered feel.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious, editorial layout with ample breathing room. Navigation items have generous horizontal padding and are spaced apart, avoiding a cramped appearance. The hero headline uses large type and generous line spacing, with no additional UI clutter (no buttons or subtitles visible), resulting in a low content density and a calm, premium presentation.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Primary flow is a strong vertical path: the eye first hits the high-contrast teal logo at the top left, then moves horizontally across the navigation bar and the standout teal 'FRANÇAIS' button. From there, the large background image and central alignment encourage the eye to drop down into the hero, focusing on the oversized 'MORE THAN PRETTY SINCE 1990' headline. The blurred background forms a soft frame that keeps attention anchored on the central text. Overall, it’s a hybrid of an initial horizontal scan across the header followed by a strong vertical focus on the hero center.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Rectangular with subtle or no corner rounding (approximately 2–4px radius, visually close to sharp corners). Solid fill in the brand teal (#4E8F9B) with high-contrast white, all-caps sans-serif text. No obvious shadow; it appears flat and modern. Vertical padding is moderate, with more horizontal padding for a pill-like proportion, but edges remain mostly straight. Likely no borders beyond the fill color.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Implemented as either a <button> element or an <a> link styled as a button, typically inside the navigation bar container. Expected structure: a single text node in uppercase, possibly wrapped in a span for additional styling. For language toggle, it may be an <a href> link styled as a primary button.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Not directly visible in the screenshot, but consistent with the aesthetic they are likely flat, clean rectangles with either a very subtle border (#E0E0E0–#EEEEEE) or a soft shadow (e.g., 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)). Corners likely match the button’s subtle rounding (2–4px). Cards for models would likely feature full-width imagery on top with text below, generous padding (16–24px), and minimal ornamentation.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Typical structure would be a container
with an image element at the top ( or ), followed by text blocks: model name in a heading tag (h3/h4) and meta info in paragraphs or small text. Layout likely uses flexbox or CSS grid for card grids, with each card as a column item.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Not shown, but inferred as simple, clean fields with full-width rectangular inputs, light border (e.g., 1px solid #CCCCCC) on white background, and no heavy shadows. Labels likely set above inputs in small, uppercase or title-case sans-serif. Error or validation states would be indicated by border color changes (e.g., red) and small helper text beneath fields. Spacing between fields would be moderate (12–20px).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms likely use stacked labels with full-width inputs inside a vertical form layout. Each field group would be a <div> containing a <label> followed by an <input> or <textarea>. For multi-column desktop layouts (e.g., application forms), CSS grid or flexbox may place two fields side-by-side on larger screens, but fall back to a single column on mobile.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Horizontal, top-fixed (or at least top-positioned) navigation bar on a white background. Logo area on the left is a solid teal rectangular block with white serif text and a small sans-serif tagline strip. Nav items are uppercase or small caps sans-serif in dark gray or black, evenly spaced with equal padding and no separating lines. Links are likely simple text with a subtle hover state (color change to teal and/or underline). The active page may be indicated by teal text color or a subtle underline. The language switch is styled as a filled teal button, visually separated from text links.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: A semantic <nav> element with an unordered list (<ul>) of <li><a> items for each navigation link. Logo block likely an <a> link wrapping the brand name. The language switch is another <a> inside the nav, possibly with an additional class (e.g., .btn-primary) for button styling. Flexbox is used to align logo and nav items horizontally and to push the nav + button to the right.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Inferred patterns for lists of models or news: likely either grid-style tiles (for models) or clean stacked rows (for news). For grid lists, items would have generous spacing between cards with clear separation via white space, not heavy borders. For stacked lists, each row would have comfortable vertical padding and possibly a light divider line or subtle background contrast on hover. Thumbnail images (for models) would be prominent, with text secondary beneath or to the right.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: For model listings: a CSS grid or flexbox container with each item as a card-like <div>, including an image and text. For textual lists (news or services): an unordered list or a series of <article> or <div> elements stacked vertically, each containing a heading, date/meta area, and summary text. Alignment would favor left-aligned text with consistent baseline rhythm.

heading.visual_style: High-contrast, elegant serif reminiscent of fashion magazines (Didone style) with thin hairlines and thick verticals, strong contrast, and sharp serifs. Used in all caps for the logo and hero headline, giving a formal and luxurious feel.

body.visual_style: Neutral, clean sans-serif for navigation and likely body copy. It appears unobtrusive and modern, closer to a classic Helvetica-like grotesque than a highly rounded or decorative style.

spacing_system.component_padding: Buttons and navigation items: approximately 8px vertical and 16–24px horizontal padding. Cards and content blocks: around 16–24px internal padding on all sides to maintain an airy feel.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., hero to next section) likely in the 48–64px range on desktop, maintaining large breaks between content areas.

interaction_hints: Navigation link hover states likely switch text color to the brand teal (#4E8F9B) and/or add an underline for clarity.
The 'FRANÇAIS' button likely uses a hover darkening or lightening of the teal background and possibly a slight change in box-shadow or border to indicate interactivity.
Hero text may fade or slide in on page load using subtle opacity and translateY transitions to reinforce the premium, editorial feel without being distracting.
Future cards for models or news could scale images slightly or lift with a subtle shadow on hover to indicate clickability.
Language switch and navigation buttons may have a focused outline or shadow to improve keyboard accessibility and indicate active/focus states.
